characters.name: Benjamin Bunny
characters.description: Peter Rabbit's cousin. A confident young rabbit wearing a tam-o-shanter cap and a brown jacket. Bold and adventurous, he takes the lead.
characters.source: Benjamin Bunny from Beatrix Potter's The Tale of Benjamin Bunny (1904)

characters.name: Peter Rabbit
characters.description: A young rabbit, Benjamin's cousin. At the start he has lost his blue jacket and shoes (from his previous adventure) and is wrapped in a red cotton handkerchief, miserable. Later recovers his blue jacket.
characters.source: Peter Rabbit from Beatrix Potter's The Tale of Peter Rabbit (1902)

characters.name: Mrs. Rabbit
characters.description: Peter's mother. Wears an apron and blue dress. Runs a small shop under a fir tree.
characters.source: Mrs. Rabbit (Old Mrs. Rabbit) from Beatrix Potter's Peter Rabbit series

characters.name: Mr. Bouncer
characters.description: Benjamin's father, old Mr. Benjamin Bunny. A large, stern old rabbit in a purple-brown jacket who smokes a pipe and carries a switch. The authority figure who rescues and punishes.
characters.source: Old Mr. Benjamin Bunny from Beatrix Potter's The Tale of Benjamin Bunny

characters.name: The Cat
characters.description: Mr. McGregor's large tabby cat. Sleeps in the garden and traps the rabbits under a basket.
characters.source: The cat from Beatrix Potter's The Tale of Benjamin Bunny

locations.name: The Rabbit Burrow
locations.description: The rabbit family home under a big fir tree. Mrs. Rabbit's shop where she sells tea and tobacco. Cozy interior with herbs hanging from the ceiling.

locations.name: The Woods
locations.description: Woodland path between the burrow and the garden. Trees, mossy banks, fallen leaves.

locations.name: The Garden Wall
locations.description: The high stone wall around Mr. McGregor's garden. The rabbits climb up and look down into the garden from here.

locations.name: Mr. McGregor's Garden
locations.description: The same vegetable garden from Peter Rabbit. Rows of lettuce, onions, vegetables. Has a scarecrow, a garden shed, flowerpots, and a wooden gate.

pages.english_text: Peter sat alone. He had no jacket and no shoes.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_003.jpg] Peter sitting sadly on a mossy bank in the woods. He is bare — no blue jacket, no shoes. He looks forlorn and cold after his adventure in Mr. McGregor's garden.

pages.english_text: His mother wrapped him in a red handkerchief.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_007.jpg] Peter wrapped tightly in a big red cotton pocket-handkerchief. He looks small and miserable. His lost shoes are visible nearby.

pages.english_text: Benjamin Bunny was Peter's cousin. He was very bold.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_004.jpg] Benjamin Bunny walking confidently through the woods, wearing his tam-o-shanter cap and brown jacket. He walks upright with a spring in his step. A figure visible in the background.

pages.english_text: Benjamin found Peter. "Why are you sad?" he asked.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_008.jpg] Benjamin whispering to Peter, who is still wrapped in the red handkerchief. Benjamin leans in close, concerned but confident. Peter looks up at him.

pages.english_text: "I lost my jacket in the garden," said Peter.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_006.jpg] Peter sitting hunched in the handkerchief, looking down. He is explaining what happened — he lost his clothes to the scarecrow in McGregor's garden.

pages.english_text: "Come!" said Benjamin. "We will get it back!"
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_009.jpg] Benjamin leading Peter (still in the red handkerchief) along the path toward the garden. Benjamin walks ahead confidently; Peter follows nervously.

pages.english_text: They climbed up the garden wall and looked down.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_010.jpg] Benjamin and Peter on top of the high garden wall, looking down into Mr. McGregor's garden. The scarecrow wearing Peter's blue jacket is visible below.

pages.english_text: Peter fell down from the wall! He tumbled into the garden.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_011.jpg] Peter (still in red handkerchief) tumbling and falling off the wall into the garden. Benjamin watches from the wall above. A comic, clumsy fall.

pages.english_text: They saw Peter's jacket on the scarecrow!
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_013.jpg] The scarecrow dressed in Peter's blue jacket and shoes. Benjamin is taking back the tam-o-shanter hat. Birds perch nearby watching. The moment of finding the lost clothes.

pages.english_text: Benjamin took the jacket off the scarecrow. Peter put it on.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_012.jpg] Benjamin and Peter standing in the garden. Peter is now wearing his blue jacket again. Benjamin stands beside him. Footprints visible on the ground.

pages.english_text: Then they picked onions to fill their basket.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_014.jpg] Peter (in blue jacket) and Benjamin among rows of large onions in the garden. They are gathering onions, filling a handkerchief-basket. Vegetables all around them.

pages.english_text: Benjamin ate some lettuce. He was not afraid at all.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_015.jpg] Benjamin munching lettuce leaves among the lettuces in the garden. Peter peeks from behind the lettuce. Benjamin is relaxed and confident — no fear.

pages.english_text: Peter kept dropping the onions. He was nervous!
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_016.jpg] Peter in his blue jacket holding the red handkerchief bundle, but onions spilling out and rolling on the ground. He looks anxious and clumsy.

pages.english_text: Peter heard a noise. "We must go!" he said.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_019.jpg] Peter and Benjamin in the garden shed area, both alert. Peter's ears are up — he heard something. Watering cans and pots around them. Tension building.

pages.english_text: Then they saw the cat! She was very big.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_020.jpg] The two rabbits peering around a corner and seeing the large tabby cat lying in the garden path. The cat is big and intimidating. The rabbits freeze.

pages.english_text: They hid under a basket. The cat sat on top!
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_022.jpg] The big tabby cat sleeping contentedly on top of a woven basket. Inside, hidden beneath, are Peter and Benjamin — trapped. A tense, scary moment.

pages.english_text: They waited and waited. They could not move.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_021.jpg] The cat sniffing at the basket from the side. The rabbits are trapped inside. Time passes. They are stuck and frightened.

pages.english_text: Then old Mr. Bouncer came! He was Benjamin's father.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_023.jpg] Old Mr. Bouncer — a large, stern rabbit in a purple-brown jacket, smoking a pipe, carrying a switch — striding purposefully through the garden. He has come looking for his son.

pages.english_text: Mr. Bouncer jumped on the cat! She ran away!
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_024.jpg] Mr. Bouncer leaping onto the startled cat, who recoils in surprise. A dramatic, decisive rescue. The old rabbit is fierce and brave.

pages.english_text: He pulled Benjamin out by the ears. Benjamin was in trouble.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_025.jpg] Mr. Bouncer grabbing Benjamin firmly by the ears and scolding him. Peter watches nearby looking small. Benjamin is being punished for going into the garden.

pages.english_text: Mr. Bouncer marched them all home. Out the gate!
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_026.jpg] Mr. Bouncer marching Benjamin and Peter out through the garden gate, carrying the basket of onions. The cat watches from behind. A stern procession home.

pages.english_text: Peter gave the onions to his mother. He had his jacket back!
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_028.jpg] Mrs. Rabbit at home in her burrow with the young bunnies. Peter, back in his blue jacket, arrives with the handkerchief bundle of onions. A warm homecoming — all is well.
